name: recommendation
purpose: Rank candidate options against weighted goals and hard constraints, evidence-weighted, with why-not for the alternatives — decision SUPPORT, never a decision.

transformation.statement: Candidate Options + Decision Criteria (+ Context, + KB evidence) -> ranked recommendations with why-not; decision-support, never a decision

consumes.name: options
consumes.description: The candidate options to rank (>=2). Composable: a Search result set or a Combination-Discovery candidate set adapts to this.

options.description: Cannot recommend without alternatives to compare — a single option is not a recommendation.

ref.description: Handle of the option (product/material/approach) in the knowledge graph.

consumes.name: criteria
consumes.description: Weighted goals (the Value Field) + hard constraints. Ranking is meaningless without objectives.

weights.description: Goal -> weight (0..1), e.g. { fire: 0.5, cost: 0.3, voc: 0.2 }.

consumes.name: context
consumes.description: Optional project/geo/application context that conditions the ranking.

produces.name: recommendations
produces.description: Ranked options with rationale. Safety is enforced structurally: every recommendation REQUIRES trade-offs, why-not vs. alternatives, missing evidence, confidence, assumptions, and a decisionStatus locked to 'decision_support'. The engine returns a ranking; the human decides.

alternativesConsidered.description: Every option scored — so why-not is always visible, not just the winner.

rationale.description: Why this ranks where it does, in terms of the weighted goals.

confidenceType.description: Evidence-weighted; constraint-satisfaction is shown in the ranking breakdown, not folded into the number. See gap RG2.

decisionStatus.description: HARD-LOCKED. A recommendation is never a decision (see gap RG3 — the manifest enum lacks a 'decision' token, so it is enforced here in the payload).

tradeOffs.description: What you give up for this choice (e.g. '+fire, -workability, +cost').

whyNotAlternatives.description: Why the other options rank lower — every recommendation exposes its rejected alternatives.

missingEvidence.description: Evidence that would raise/lower confidence but is absent (feeds Knowledge Gap / Experiment Planner).

provenance.description: The KB evidence behind the ranking.

reasoning.evidenceModel: each option scored by evidence weight per goal, combined under the criteria weights; constraint-aware (hard constraints gate, soft goals rank).
reasoning.notes: PROXIES: reasoning.class 'optimization' stands in for a 'recommendation'/'deliberative' class the frozen v1.0 enum lacks (gap RG1); confidenceType 'independent_evidence' stands in for an 'evidence_weighted'/'multi_criteria' type (gap RG2). Both are honest fits, not distortions. Uncertainty is never hidden — ties are reported, not broken.

confidence.notes: Per-recommendation confidence reflects how well evidence supports that option's ranking under the given goals. Low evidence -> low confidence + missingEvidence populated, never a confident guess.

cost.notes: Scores N options against the KB evidence per goal. Linear in options x goals.

latency.notes: Unmeasured; dominated by per-option evidence lookups.

dependencies.name: IKL performance + failure library
dependencies.notes: Evidence per option (measured vs claimed, known failures).

dependencies.name: IKL regulatory + value-engineering
dependencies.notes: For constraint checks and cost goals.

failureModes.result: rejected — cannot rank without objectives (will not invent goals)

failureModes.condition: all options fail a hard constraint
failureModes.result: empty ranked set + explanation (not an error)

failureModes.condition: options lack evidence to rank confidently
failureModes.result: low-confidence recommendations with missingEvidence populated — uncertainty exposed, never hidden

failureModes.condition: top options tie within the confidence interval
failureModes.result: returns the tied set flagged — does NOT break the tie arbitrarily (never auto-decide)

provenance.notes: Every ranking cites the evidence behind it; a recommendation without provenance is structurally impossible (required in produces schema).
